title: Limited recourse borrowing arrangements
description: How to set up limited recourse borrowing arrangements, and the different superannuation laws and rules that apply.
keywords: limited recourse borrowing arrangements, SMSF borrowing arrangements

child_links.title: About LRBAs
child_links.description: What an LRBA is and what you need to consider before entering into one.

child_links.title: Rules for entering an LRBA
child_links.description: The rules your self-managed super fund (SMSF) must follow depends on when you entered the LRBA.

child_links.title: Relationships with the LRBA lender
child_links.description: Understanding whether the relationship between loan and lender creates any compliance issues under superannuation law.

child_links.title: Rules on assets under LRBA
child_links.description: SMSF trustees must consider how superannuation law applies to the asset held by the holding trust under the LRBA.

child_links.title: In-house asset rules for LRBAs
child_links.description: Certain exceptions apply to the in-house asset rules for LRBAs, as the holding trust is a related party.

child_links.title: LRBAs and total superannuation balance
child_links.description: How to work out a member's share of the outstanding balance of an LRBA for their total superannuation balance.
